content:
# Ruth Chapter 3 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 881-882 | **Source Image:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/RUT_003_page_001.jpg, RUT_003_page_002.jpg

---

**3:1** And-said to-her Naomi her-mother-in-law, "My-daughter, shall-I-not seek for-you rest, that it-may-be-well with-you?

**3:2** And-now, is-not Boaz our-kinsman, whom you-were with his-young-women? Behold, he [is] winnowing the-threshing-floor of-the-barley the-night.

**3:3** And-you-shall-wash, and-you-shall-anoint-yourself, and-you-shall-put your-garments upon-you, and-you-shall-go-down [to] the-threshing-floor. Do-not-make-yourself-known to-the-man until he-has-finished to-eat and-to-drink.

**3:4** And-it-shall-be when-he-lies-down, and-you-shall-know the-place where he-lies-down there, and-you-shall-come, and-you-shall-uncover his-feet, and-you-shall-lie-down. And-he will-tell to-you that which you-shall-do."

**3:5** And-she-said to-her, "All which you-say to-me I-will-do."

**3:6** And-she-went-down [to] the-threshing-floor, and-she-did according-to-all which commanded-her her-mother-in-law.

**3:7** And-Boaz ate, and-he-drank, and-his-heart was-good, and-he-went to-lie-down at-[the]-end of-the-heap. And-she-came in-secret, and-she-uncovered his-feet, and-she-lay-down.

**3:8** And-it-was in-[the]-middle of-the-night, and-trembled the-man, and-he-turned-himself, and-behold, a-woman [was] lying-down at his-feet.

**3:9** And-he-said, "Who [are] you?" And-she-said, "I [am] Ruth your-maidservant. And-you-shall-spread your-wing [kanaf] over your-maidservant, for you [are] a-kinsman-redeemer [go'el]."

**3:10** And-he-said, "Blessed [are] you to-YHWH, my-daughter. You-have-made-good your-latter lovingkindness [hesed] more-than the-former, to-not go after the-young-men, whether poor or-whether rich.

**3:11** And-now, my-daughter, do-not-fear. All which you-say I-will-do for-you, for all the-gate of-my-people knows that a-woman of-valor [eshet hayil] you [are].

**3:12** And-now, indeed truly, I [am] a-kinsman-redeemer [go'el]. And-also there-is a-kinsman-redeemer [go'el] nearer than-I.

**3:13** Lodge the-night, and-it-shall-be in-the-morning, if he-will-redeem-you [yig'alekh], good, let-him-redeem. And-if he-does-not-desire to-redeem-you, then-I-will-redeem-you, I, [as] YHWH lives. Lie-down until the-morning."

**3:14** And-she-lay-down at his-feet until the-morning, and-she-rose before a-man could-recognize his-companion. And-he-said, "Let-it-not be-known that the-woman came [to] the-threshing-floor."

**3:15** And-he-said, "Give the-shawl which [is] upon-you, and-hold-it." And-she-held-it, and-he-measured six [measures of] barley, and-he-placed [it] upon-her. And-he-went [to] the-city.

**3:16** And-she-came to her-mother-in-law, and-she-said, "Who [are] you, my-daughter?" And-she-told to-her all which the-man did to-her.

**3:17** And-she-said, "These six [measures of] the-barley he-gave to-me, for he-said to-me, 'Do-not-go empty to your-mother-in-law.'"

**3:18** And-she-said, "Sit, my-daughter, until you-know how the-matter will-fall, for the-man will-not rest except he-has-finished the-matter today."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **3:3 -- Ketiv/Qere**: The Leningrad Codex has a notable Ketiv/Qere in this verse. The written text (Ketiv) reads וְיָרַדְתְּ (veyaradti, "and-I-will-go-down," 1st person) but the Qere reading is וְיָרַדְתְּ (veyaradti, "and-you-shall-go-down," 2nd person feminine). The context clearly requires the 2nd person, and EMET follows the Qere.

2. **3:5 -- Ketiv/Qere**: The Ketiv reads אֵלַי ("to-me") but the Qere reads אֵלָי ("to-me") -- a minor vocalization variant preserved in the Masoretic apparatus.

3. **3:9 -- "wing"** (כְּנָפֶךָ): Ruth's request that Boaz spread his "wing" (kanaf) over her deliberately echoes Boaz's own words in 2:12 about YHWH's "wings" (k'nafav). Ruth is asking Boaz to become the human agent of the divine protection he himself invoked. The word כָּנָף also means "skirt/garment-edge," creating a double meaning.

4. **3:10 -- "latter hesed more than the former"**: Boaz identifies two acts of lovingkindness (hesed) by Ruth: the "former" (her loyalty to Naomi, ch. 1) and the "latter" (her choosing Boaz as redeemer rather than pursuing younger men). This is the second of three uses of hesed in Ruth (1:8, 2:20, 3:10).

5. **3:11 -- eshet hayil** (אֵשֶׁת חַיִל): "Woman of valor." This phrase connects Ruth to Proverbs 31:10 and forms an inclusio with the description of Boaz as gibbor hayil ("mighty of valor") in 2:1. The same root חַיִל brackets both characters.

6. **3:12 -- "indeed truly"** (כִּי אָמְנָם): An emphatic double particle reinforcing the truth of what follows -- both that Boaz is a go'el and that a nearer go'el exists.

7. **3:13 -- Oath formula**: "As YHWH lives" (חַי יְהוָה) is a solemn oath formula. Boaz swears by the life of YHWH to fulfill the redemption if the nearer kinsman declines.

8. **3:15 -- Ketiv/Qere**: The Ketiv reads וַיָּבֹא (vayyavo', "and-he-went," masculine) but many manuscripts read וַתָּבֹא (vattavo', "and-she-went," feminine). The Leningrad Codex preserves the masculine reading. EMET follows the LC text: "and-he-went [to] the-city."

9. **3:16 -- "Who are you?"** (מִי אַתְּ): Naomi's question likely means "How did it go?" or "What [happened to] you?" rather than a literal identity question. The literal rendering is preserved.

-- EMET
